examples.title: Algorithm: IoT Level Data Processing Flow
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating data flow and processing at different IoT levels from simple single-node to complex edge-cloud hybrid
examples.code:
ALGORITHM IoT_Data_Processing_Level(InputData, Level)
INPUT: sensor_data, target_iot_level
OUTPUT: processed_result, storage_location

BEGIN
    CASE Level OF
        
        WHEN 1 THEN:
            // Level 1: Single Node - All processing on device
            sensor_reading <- READ_SENSOR()
            processed <- PROCESS_LOCAL(sensor_reading)
            ACTUATE(processed)
            RETURN processed, "device"
            
        WHEN 2 THEN:
            // Level 2: Single Node with Local Storage
            sensor_reading <- READ_SENSOR()
            STORE_LOCAL(sensor_reading, "local_db")
            processed <- PROCESS_LOCAL(sensor_reading)
            RETURN processed, "local_storage"
            
        WHEN 3 THEN:
            // Level 3: Single Node with Local Analytics
            sensor_reading <- READ_SENSOR()
            STORE_LOCAL(sensor_reading, "local_db")
            analytics_result <- ANALYZE_LOCAL(sensor_reading)
            IF analytics_result.threshold_exceeded THEN
                ACTUATE(analytics_result.action)
            END IF
            RETURN analytics_result, "edge"
            
        WHEN 4 THEN:
            // Level 4: Multiple Nodes with Central Aggregation
            nodes <- GET_ALL_SENSOR_NODES()
            aggregated_data <- []
            FOR EACH node IN nodes DO
                data <- COLLECT_FROM_NODE(node)
                aggregated_data.APPEND(data)
            END FOR
            STORE_LOCAL(aggregated_data, "hub_storage")
            processed <- PROCESS_AT_HUB(aggregated_data)
            RETURN processed, "central_node"
            
        WHEN 5 THEN:
            // Level 5: Multiple Nodes with Cloud Storage
            nodes <- GET_ALL_SENSOR_NODES()
            FOR EACH node IN nodes DO
                data <- COLLECT_FROM_NODE(node)
                SEND_TO_CLOUD(data)
            END FOR
            cloud_result <- PROCESS_IN_CLOUD(all_data)
            RETURN cloud_result, "cloud"
            
        WHEN 6 THEN:
            // Level 6: Hybrid Edge + Cloud Computing
            sensor_reading <- READ_SENSOR()
            // Edge processing for real-time response
            edge_result <- PROCESS_AT_EDGE(sensor_reading)
            IF edge_result.requires_cloud THEN
                // Send to cloud for advanced analytics
                SEND_TO_CLOUD(sensor_reading)
                cloud_analytics <- ANALYZE_IN_CLOUD(sensor_reading)
                edge_result.enhance(cloud_analytics)
            END IF
            RETURN edge_result, "hybrid"
            
    END CASE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates how data processing flows differently across all six IoT levels. Level 1 is simplest with all processing on a single device. Levels 2-3 add local storage and analytics capabilities. Level 4 introduces multiple nodes with central aggregation. Level 5 uses cloud-centric processing. Level 6 combines edge and cloud for hybrid processing. Each level shows where data is stored, processed, and how decisions are made.

examples.title: Python: IoT Levels and Deployment Simulation
examples.description: Python simulation demonstrating all six IoT levels and deployment templates with data processing patterns
examples.code:
"""
IoT Levels and Deployment Templates Simulation
Demonstrates all 6 IoT levels and various deployment patterns
"""
import time
from enum import Enum
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime

class IoTLevel(Enum):
    LEVEL_1_SINGLE_NODE = "Level 1: Single Node"
    LEVEL_2_LOCAL_STORAGE = "Level 2: Single Node + Local Storage"
    LEVEL_3_EDGE_ANALYTICS = "Level 3: Single Node + Edge Analytics"
    LEVEL_4_MULTIPLE_NODES = "Level 4: Multiple Nodes + Central""
    LEVEL_5_CLOUD_STORAGE = "Level 5: Multiple Nodes + Cloud"
    LEVEL_6_HYBRID = "Level 6: Hybrid Edge + Cloud"

class DeploymentTemplate(Enum):
    CLOUD_CENTRIC = "Cloud-Centric"
    EDGE_CENTRIC = "Edge-Centric"
    FOG_COMPUTING = "Fog Computing"
    HYBRID = "Hybrid"

@dataclass
class SensorData:
    node_id: str
    value: float
    timestamp: datetime
    processed: bool = False

class IoTNode:
    """Represents a single IoT device/node"""
    def __init__(self, node_id: str, has_analytics: bool = False):
        self.node_id = node_id
        self.has_analytics = has_analytics
        self.local_storage = []
    
    def read_sensor(self) -> SensorData:
        """Simulate sensor reading"""
        return SensorData(
            node_id=self.node_id,
            value=round(20 + (hash(self.node_id) % 30), 1),
            timestamp=datetime.now()
        )
    
    def process_locally(self, data: SensorData) -> float:
        """Level 1-3: Local processing"""
        processed_value = data.value * 1.1  # Simple processing
        return processed_value
    
    def analyze_locally(self, data: SensorData) -> Dict:
        """Level 3: Edge analytics"""
        return {
            'alert': data.value > 40,
            'value': data.value,
            'action': 'ACTUATE' if data.value > 40 else 'WAIT'
        }
    
    def store_locally(self, data: SensorData):
        """Level 2-3: Local storage"""
        self.local_storage.append(data)

class CentralHub:
    """Level 4: Central aggregation node"""
    def __init__(self):
        self.connected_nodes: List[IoTNode] = []
        self.aggregated_data = []
    
    def add_node(self, node: IoTNode):
        self.connected_nodes.append(node)
    
    def aggregate_data(self) -> List[SensorData]:
        """Collect and aggregate data from all nodes"""
        self.aggregated_data = []
        for node in self.connected_nodes:
            data = node.read_sensor()
            self.aggregated_data.append(data)
        return self.aggregated_data
    
    def process_at_hub(self, data: List[SensorData]) -> Dict:
        """Process aggregated data"""
        avg_value = sum(d.value for d in data) / len(data)
        return {'average': avg_value, 'node_count': len(data)}

class CloudPlatform:
    """Cloud infrastructure for Level 5-6"""
    def __init__(self):
        self.cloud_storage = []
    
    def store_in_cloud(self, data: List[SensorData]):
        """Cloud storage"""
        self.cloud_storage.extend(data)
    
    def process_in_cloud(self, data: List[SensorData]) -> Dict:
        """Advanced cloud analytics"""
        values = [d.value for d in data]
        return {
            'min': min(values),
            'max': max(values),
            'avg': sum(values) / len(values),
            'total_records': len(self.cloud_storage)
        }

def simulate_iot_level(level: IoTLevel):
    """Simulate data processing at a specific IoT level"""
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"Simulating: {level.value}")
    print('='*50)
    
    if level == IoTLevel.LEVEL_1_SINGLE_NODE:
        # Level 1: Single device does everything
        node = IoTNode("node_1")
        data = node.read_sensor()
        result = node.process_locally(data)
        print(f"Sensor: {data.value} -> Processed: {result}")
        print(f"Storage: None (all on device)")
        
    elif level == IoTLevel.LEVEL_2_LOCAL_STORAGE:
        # Level 2: Add local storage
        node = IoTNode("node_1")
        data = node.read_sensor()
        node.store_locally(data)
        result = node.process_locally(data)
        print(f"Sensor: {data.value} -> Stored locally + Processed")
        print(f"Local records: {len(node.local_storage)}")
        
    elif level == IoTLevel.LEVEL_3_EDGE_ANALYTICS:
        # Level 3: Edge analytics
        node = IoTNode("node_1", has_analytics=True)
        data = node.read_sensor()
        node.store_locally(data)
        analytics = node.analyze_locally(data)
        print(f"Sensor: {data.value}")
        print(f"Edge Analytics: {analytics}")
        print(f"Action: {analytics['action']}")
        
    elif level == IoTLevel.LEVEL_4_MULTIPLE_NODES:
        # Level 4: Multiple nodes with central hub
        hub = CentralHub()
        for i in range(4):
            hub.add_node(IoTNode(f"sensor_{i}"))
        data = hub.aggregate_data()
        result = hub.process_at_hub(data)
        print(f"Nodes: {len(data)} sensors")
        print(f"Aggregated result: {result}")
        
    elif level == IoTLevel.LEVEL_5_CLOUD_STORAGE:
        # Level 5: Cloud-centric
        cloud = CloudPlatform()
        nodes = [IoTNode(f"sensor_{i}") for i in range(3)]
        all_data = [node.read_sensor() for node in nodes]
        cloud.store_in_cloud(all_data)
        result = cloud.process_in_cloud(all_data)
        print(f"Data sent to cloud: {len(all_data)} records")
        print(f"Cloud analytics: {result}")
        
    elif level == IoTLevel.LEVEL_6_HYBRID:
        # Level 6: Hybrid edge + cloud
        node = IoTNode("edge_node", has_analytics=True)
        cloud = CloudPlatform()
        
        # Edge processing
        data = node.read_sensor()
        edge_result = node.analyze_locally(data)
        print(f"Edge processing: {edge_result}")
        
        # Cloud sync
        cloud.store_in_cloud([data])
        cloud_result = cloud.process_in_cloud([data])
        print(f"Cloud analytics: {cloud_result}")
        print(f"Deployment: Hybrid (edge + cloud)")

def demonstrate_deployment_template(template: DeploymentTemplate):
    """Show characteristics of different deployment templates"""
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"Deployment Template: {template.value}")
    print('='*50)
    
    characteristics = {
        DeploymentTemplate.CLOUD_CENTRIC: {
            'processing': 'Cloud-based',
            'latency': 'High',
            'offline': 'No',
            'use_case': 'Big data analytics, scalable services'
        },
        DeploymentTemplate.EDGE_CENTRIC: {
            'processing': 'Device/Edge',
            'latency': 'Low',
            'offline': 'Yes',
            'use_case': 'Real-time control, IoT security'
        },
        DeploymentTemplate.FOG_COMPUTING: {
            'processing': 'Gateway/Fog Nodes',
            'latency': 'Medium',
            'offline': 'Partial',
            'use_case': 'Industrial IoT, distributed systems'
        },
        DeploymentTemplate.HYBRID: {
            'processing': 'Edge + Cloud',
            'latency': 'Adaptive',
            'offline': 'Yes',
            'use_case': 'Smart cities, complex deployments'
        }
    }
    
    props = characteristics[template]
    for key, value in props.items():
        print(f"  {key.capitalize()}: {value}")

# Main execution
if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("IoT LEVELS AND DEPLOYMENT TEMPLATES SIMULATION")
    print("=" * 60)
    
    # Simulate all IoT levels
    for level in IoTLevel:
        simulate_iot_level(level)
    
    # Demonstrate deployment templates
    for template in DeploymentTemplate:
        demonstrate_deployment_template(template)
    
    print("\n" + "="*60)
    print("Simulation Complete!")
examples.explanation: This Python simulation demonstrates all six IoT levels and deployment templates. It shows how data flows from sensors through processing stages based on the level complexity. The code includes IoTNode class for edge devices, CentralHub for Level 4 aggregation, and CloudPlatform for cloud processing. It demonstrates the progression from simple single-device processing (Level 1) to hybrid edge-cloud architectures (Level 6).
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for aggregation, O(1) for individual node operations
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for data storage at each level

examples.title: C: IoT Node Architecture Implementation
examples.description: C implementation showing IoT node architecture with sensor reading, local processing, and communication modules
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates IoT Level 1-3 concepts with a practical node architecture. It shows sensor reading, local processing, local storage (with circular buffer), and edge analytics with threshold detection. The code includes configurable storage and analytics capabilities, showing how each level adds more functionality. The demonstrate_level1/2/3 functions clearly show the progression from simple to more complex IoT architectures.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for sensor read and process, O(n) for storage operations
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is storage capacity (MAX_STORAGE)

examples.title: Java: Multi-Node IoT System with Deployment Templates
examples.description: Java implementation demonstrating Level 4-6 with multiple nodes, cloud integration, and various deployment templates
examples.explanation: This Java implementation demonstrates Level 4-6 IoT architectures with a full multi-node system. It includes IoTNode, CentralHub, CloudPlatform classes, and processing units (EdgeProcessor, CloudProcessor, FogNode). The DeploymentTemplate enum shows all four templates (Cloud-Centric, Edge-Centric, Fog Computing, Hybrid) with their specific processing patterns. The code demonstrates how data flows from multiple sensors through edge/fog/cloud processing tiers.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for data collection and processing across nodes
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storage at each tier (hub, cloud)
